name: bullmq
documentation:
# BullMQ For Python

This is the official BullMQ Python library. It is a close port of the NodeJS version of the library.
Python Queues are interoperable with NodeJS Queues, as both libraries use the same .lua scripts that
power all the functionality.

## Features

Currently, the library does not support all the features available in the NodeJS version. The following
have been ported so far:

- [ ] Add jobs to queues.

  - [x] Regular jobs.
  - [x] Delayed jobs.
  - [ ] Job priority.
  - [ ] Repeatable.

- [x] Workers
- [ ] Job events.
- [x] Job progress.
- [ ] Job retries.
- [x] Job backoff.
- [x] Getters.

## Installation

```bash
pip install bullmq
```

## Usage

```python
from bullmq import Queue

queue = Queue('my-queue')

job = await queue.add('my-job', {'foo': 'bar'})

```

## Documentation

The documentation is available at [https://docs.bullmq.io](https://docs.bullmq.io/python)

## License

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.backoff_options.BackoffOptions.type
documentation: Name of the backoff strategy.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.backoff_options.BackoffOptions.delay
documentation: Delay in milliseconds.

classes.name: bullmq.types.queue_options.QueueBaseOptions

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.queue_options.QueueBaseOptions.connection
documentation: Prefix for all queue keys.
Options for the Queue class.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.backoff
documentation: Backoff setting for automatic retries if the job fails.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.jobId
documentation:
Override the job ID - by default, the job ID is a unique
integer, but you can use this setting to override it.

If you use this option, it is up to you to ensure the
jobId is unique. If you attempt to add a job with an id that
already exists, it will not be added.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.timestamp
documentation:
Timestamp when the job was created.

@defaultValue round(time.time() * 1000)

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.delay
documentation:
An amount of milliseconds to wait until this job can be processed.
Note that for accurate delays, worker and producers
should have their clocks synchronized.

@defaultValue 0

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.attempts
documentation:
The total number of attempts to try the job until it completes.

@defaultValue 0

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.removeOnComplete
documentation:
If true, removes the job when it successfully completes
When given a number, it specifies the maximum amount of
jobs to keep, or you can provide an object specifying max
age and/or count to keep. It overrides whatever setting is used in the worker.
Default behavior is to keep the job in the completed set.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.removeOnFail
documentation:
If true, removes the job when it fails after all attempts.
When given a number, it specifies the maximum amount of
jobs to keep, or you can provide an object specifying max
age and/or count to keep. It overrides whatever setting is used in the worker.
Default behavior is to keep the job in the failed set.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.job_options.JobOptions.stackTraceLimit
documentation: Limits the amount of stack trace lines that will be recorded in the stacktrace.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.autorun
documentation:
Condition to start processor at instance creation

@default true

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.concurrency
bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.maxStalledCount
documentation:
Amount of times a job can be recovered from a stalled state
to the `wait` state. If this is exceeded, the job is moved
to `failed`.

@default 1

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.stalledInterval
documentation:
Number of milliseconds between stallness checks.

@default 30000

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.lockDuration
documentation:
Duration of the lock for the job in milliseconds. The lock represents that
a worker is processing the job. If the lock is lost, the job will be eventually
be picked up by the stalled checker and move back to wait so that another worker
can process it again.

@default 30000

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.prefix
documentation: Prefix for all queue keys.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.worker_options.WorkerOptions.connection
documentation: Options for connecting to a Redis instance.

classes.name: bullmq.queue_keys.QueueKeys
documentation: This class handles all keys parser logic.

classes.name: bullmq.types.keep_jobs.KeepJobs

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.keep_jobs.KeepJobs.age
documentation: Maximum age in seconds for job to be kept.

class_variables.name: bullmq.types.keep_jobs.KeepJobs.count
documentation: Maximum count of jobs to be kept.

Specify which jobs to keep after finishing. If both age and count are
specified, then the jobs kept will be the ones that satisfies both
properties.

A background job processor and message queue for Python based on Redis.

methods.name: bullmq.worker.Worker.getNextJob
bullmq.worker.Worker.close

Closes the worker and related redis connections.

This method waits for current jobs to finalize before returning.

classes.name: bullmq.queue.Queue

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.__init__
documentation: Initialize a connection

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.add
bullmq.queue.Queue.addBulk
documentation:
Adds an array of jobs to the queue. This method may be faster than adding
one job at a time in a sequence

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.pause
documentation:
Pauses the processing of this queue globally.

We use an atomic RENAME operation on the wait queue. Since
we have blocking calls with BRPOPLPUSH on the wait queue, as long as the queue
is renamed to 'paused', no new jobs will be processed (the current ones
will run until finalized).

Adding jobs requires a LUA script to check first if the paused list exist
and in that case it will add it there instead of the wait list.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.resume
documentation:
Resumes the processing of this queue globally.

The method reverses the pause operation by resuming the processing of the
queue.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.isPaused
documentation: Returns true if the queue is currently paused.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.getRateLimitTtl
documentation: Returns the time to live for a rate limited key in milliseconds.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.getJobLogs
bullmq.queue.Queue.obliterate
documentation:
Completely destroys the queue and all of its contents irreversibly.
This method will the *pause* the queue and requires that there are no
active jobs. It is possible to bypass this requirement, i.e. not
having active jobs using the "force" option.

Note: This operation requires to iterate on all the jobs stored in the queue
and can be slow for very large queues.

@param opts: Obliterate options.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.retryJobs
documentation: Retry all the failed or completed jobs.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.promoteJobs
documentation: Retry all the delayed jobs.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.trimEvents
documentation:
Trim the event stream to an approximately maxLength.

@param maxLength:

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.removeDeprecatedPriorityKey
documentation: Delete old priority helper key.

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.getJobCounts
documentation:
Returns the job counts for each type specified or every list/set in the queue by default.

@returns: An object, key (type) and value (count)

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.getCountsPerPriority
documentation:
Returns the number of jobs per priority.

@returns: An object, key (priority) and value (count)

methods.name: bullmq.queue.Queue.clean
documentation:
Cleans jobs from a queue. Similar to drain but keeps jobs within a certain
grace period

* @returns: Id jobs from the deleted records

methods.name: bullmq.job.Job.fromJSON

documentation:
This class represents a Job in the queue. Normally job are implicitly created when
you add a job to the queue with methods such as Queue.addJob( ... )

A Job instance is also passed to the Worker's process function.

modules.name: bullmq.scripts
documentation:
This class is used to load and execute Lua scripts.
It is a wrapper around the Redis client.
